mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations, directly compressing forward S&P 500 returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in the positive direction over a 3-year rolling window, or forward returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero during rate hikes.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden spike in the unemployment rate above 4.5% signals a deteriorating labor market that reduces consumer spending expectations, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive in over 65% of instances where UNRATE crosses 4.5%, or the signal shows no predictive power in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicate a broad-based demand contraction that forces equity repricing downward within one month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce negative forward returns more than 50% of the time, or individual series predict returns independently without interaction effects.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a post-recession recovery phase signals strong corporate earnings momentum, generating positive short-term S&P 500 returns over five trading days.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative forward returns in over 60% of triggered instances during recovery phases, or loses statistical significance when filtered by regime.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding real GDP growth sustains corporate revenue trajectories, supporting positive equity market performance over a three-month horizon through fundamental earnings channels.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are negative in over 55% of periods with positive GDP growth, or the correlation between GDP growth and equity returns falls below 0.1.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price inflation triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, causing immediate negative market repricing over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: Market returns remain positive in over 60% of high-inflation months, or the threshold fails to separate return distributions significantly.

mechanism_narrative: Strong nonfarm payroll growth combined with a falling unemployment rate creates a tight labor market that boosts consumer confidence and drives positive equity returns over one month.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal yields negative forward returns in over 55% of observations, or the interaction term adds no explanatory power beyond individual series.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle signals supply-side constraints that erode corporate margins, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in over 60% of tightening periods with falling industrial production, or the regime filter fails to improve out-of-sample accuracy.
